scenario.context: On-call paged at 3:14am. Datadog alert: 47 failed logins followed by 1 successful login from 198.51.100.42 to admin@quillfinance.io. Admin role has access to payment processing config and the customer card vault.

log_lines.text: Indicators: 1 IP, 1 email, login burst pattern
Pattern matching: 47 failed → 1 success = brute force / credential stuffing
Time of day: 3:14am UTC (off-hours, low legit traffic)
Affected role: admin (privileged, payment vault access)
Severity computed: P1 (critical) — privileged account compromise possible
AbuseIPDB: 142 reports in last 30 days, score 96/100
Categories: Brute-Force, SSH, Web App Attack, Bad Web Bot
AlienVault OTX: appears in 3 active pulses (credential stuffing operations)
Verdict: MALICIOUS — block at WAF immediately
Have I Been Pwned: 4 breaches found
Hypothesis confirmed: admin password likely reused from breach corpus

reveals.html:
⚠ P1 INCIDENT
Credential stuffing — privileged account compromise likely
admin@quillfinance.io · 198.51.100.42 (Moscow, RU) · 3:14am UTC · confidence 92%
Severity
P1
IP Reputation
96/100
Account in Breaches
4
Response SLA
15 min

High confidence the attacker successfully authenticated. Containment must complete in the next 15 minutes before the attacker pivots from admin role into the payment vault.

⏱ Containment plan — execute in this order

Each step is sequenced to limit attacker dwell time without destroying forensic evidence.

0–2 min
1. Force-revoke active session for admin@quillfinance.io
In Okta admin console: User Profile → Active Sessions → Revoke. This kicks the attacker out of any existing session without alerting them via password reset.
okta.quillfinance.io/admin/users/00u****J9
2–5 min
2. Block 198.51.100.42 at the Cloudflare WAF
Add deny rule for source IP. Also consider blocking the /24 (198.51.100.0/24) since stuffing operations rotate IPs in adjacent ranges.
Cloudflare → Security → WAF → Custom Rules
5–8 min
3. Audit recent admin actions in CloudTrail
Pull all API calls from admin@quillfinance.io's session in the last 4 hours. Look for: payment config changes, IAM role grants, S3 reads from card-vault bucket, secret rotations.
aws cloudtrail lookup-events --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=Username,AttributeValue=admin@quillfinance.io
8–12 min
4. Force password reset + MFA re-enrollment
After session revoke and audit. Issue temporary password via secure channel (in-person or Signal), require MFA re-enrollment with a hardware key.
Okta → User Profile → Reset Password (admin-initiated)
12–15 min
5. Preserve forensic evidence before any cleanup
Snapshot Okta system log, CloudTrail JSON dumps, WAF logs, and Datadog audit timeline. Store in s3://quillfinance-incidents/2026-05-03/.
Required for post-incident review and any law enforcement coordination

📨 Communication templates
Internal — Slack #incident-response
🚨 P1 SECURITY INCIDENT — credential stuffing on admin account What: Successful login to admin@quillfinance.io after 47 failed attempts from a known-malicious IP (Moscow, RU). When: 3:14am UTC, ongoing. Scope: admin role has payment config + card vault read access. IC: @oncall-security · Comms: @cto · Legal on standby. Updates every 15 min. Containment plan running, ETA 15 min.
Customer notification — only if cardholder data accessed
Subject: Security update from Quill Finance We are investigating a security event affecting one of our internal admin accounts. At this time we have no evidence that customer data was accessed. We will provide an update within 24 hours. As a precaution, we recommend reviewing your account activity at app.quillfinance.io/security.
Regulatory — only if PCI scope confirmed breached
Subject: Suspected security event — Quill Finance To: payment_card_brand_security@[brand].com · acquiring_bank_security@[bank].com Quill Finance is investigating a suspected unauthorized access to systems within our PCI scope. Initial indicators suggest credential compromise of a privileged administrator account at 03:14 UTC on [date]. We are following our incident response plan and will provide a formal notification within the contractual timeframe.

What your agent did
// Datadog → triggers a webhook to your agent at 3:14am // Agent: "Triage this security alert and tell me what to do" const triage = await mcp.call("incident_triage", { incident_description: "47 failed logins followed by 1 successful login to admin account from Russia at 3am", indicators: { suspicious_ips: ["198.51.100.42"], affected_accounts: ["admin@quillfinance.io"], log_snippets: [""] }, environment_context: { has_edr: true, has_siem: true, has_backups: true, has_incident_response_plan: true } }); // Agent: "Look up the IP and check if the email is in any breaches" const [intel, breach] = await Promise.all([ mcp.call("threat_intel_lookup", { indicator: "198.51.100.42", indicator_type: "ip" }), mcp.call("credential_check", { check_type: "email", target: "admin@quillfinance.io" }) ]); // Total cost: $1.50 (triage $0.75 + intel $0.25 + breach $0.50) · ~5 seconds wall clock // Output: containment plan + comm templates, all delivered to on-call's Slack DM
